open_trace.regex: workbook opened \(no sensitivity label detected; encryption: not enabled\)

provenance_note.regex: generated nightly by upstream Python script

children.judgment_q: Does the workbook carry a Microsoft Purview / AIP sensitivity label whose policy excludes the Robot identity (interactive admin can open; Robot service cannot)?
Does the configured Range reference a defined name (named range) whose Name Manager 'Refers to' shows #REF!/#NAME?/#VALUE!, or a formula with a #REF! reference? Requires Ctrl+F3 inspection.

nodes.reason: Stack inside DocumentFormat.OpenXml.* narrows to branch 2 (structural corruption) vs branch 4 (unsupported OpenXML feature) — not distinguishable by wording. Hand the user the corruption check (Excel 'Repaired' dialog / provenance) AND the Compatibility-Checker check.

Stack inside Microsoft.Office.Interop.Excel.* (COMException) narrows to branch 4 (COM parse) vs branch 5 (COM memory limit). Hand the user the Compatibility-Checker check and the size/populated-cell check (>100 MB or >1M cells).

Opaque path confirmed: plain NRE/TIE from inside UiPath.Excel.Activities.*, no label (branch 1 out), used-range not a named range (branch 3 out). Remaining branches 2/4/5 are NOT CLI-distinguishable. Present the workbook-inspection checklist (size/LastWriteTime; open in Excel -> 'Repaired' dialog [b2]; Check Compatibility [b4]; populated-cell count [b5]) and STOP guessing. Strong corruption signal: the .xlsx is 'generated nightly by upstream Python script' (openpyxl-class writer) — prioritize the branch-2 check.

nodes.cause: Branch 1 — Microsoft Purview / AIP sensitivity label blocks programmatic access for the Robot identity.
Branch 3 — broken named range / formula reference (#REF! defined name or formula referencing a deleted sheet/cell).
Not this playbook — stack origin in System.IO.* points at file-not-found / file-locked. Re-route.
Not this playbook — stack origin in ReadRangeImpl.ResolveSheet points at sheet-not-found. Re-route.
